how are dead mnemo citations stripped from a node body

how does the code identity migration dry run copy the database

the lookup keeps coming back with nothing at all even though i am certain the information was saved somewhere - what should i work through to find out why
note: v1.2 silent-zero-hit diagnostic ladder (isolation / budget floor / bad project override)

prompt: i pointed the tool at the wrong folder and then took it back off the list but everything it slurped in is still hanging around
note: v1.1.1 lifecycle-cleanup-must-cascade lesson (+ the one-shot orphan prune for pre-fix damage)

prompt: why would the exact same project folder end up with two different identities depending on which machine the client happens to be running on
note: Path.resolve() is platform-dependent; pass through absolute paths in cross-platform key derivation

prompt: each attempt to improve how this feature behaves ends up breaking something different - should i keep going or settle for the older rougher version that actually worked
note: user-validated resolution rule: revert to last known-good beats an extended perfectionize that regresses

prompt: i checked the display problem on my own setup and it looked correct but the person who reported it insists nothing has changed
note: reproduce the reporter's exact scenario (their conversation, width, panel state) before claiming a UI fix

prompt: do i really need to run the full checks for a change that only edits written prose
note: docs-only commits still break the suite; the doc tests assert a closed set of README links

prompt: the official way to detect that someone clicked blank space instead of an item stops working once the library ships in its compressed form
note: reference equality against a library's core breaks under minification; use a capability test instead

prompt: why do notes written while working on one codebase stay out of another codebase's results by default, and how do i mark something as applying everywhere
note: project-isolation-by-default + the BASE escape hatch (strategic principle, 2026-05-10)

prompt: what do we offer that a rival product built only around source code cannot
note: mnemo's durable positioning vs code-only intelligence tools

prompt: what should we actually measure to know this thing is dependable if search ranking scores are not it
note: v6.0.0 reliability arc -- hit@k is the wrong bar; measure agentic task-success over the moat

prompt: is blending the two search rankings by their positions better than leaning on the stronger one
note: 2026-06-18 measured fusion verdict -- naive RRF loses to BM25 alone; the 6-term sum under-weights lexical

prompt: what stops an assistant from saying it ran the checks when it never actually did
note: v6.1.0 G3 evidence ledger -- real exit code captured passively; freshness re-opens the gate on any edit

prompt: why not just hand the model the whole notes file and let it work it out
note: v1 token-cost doctrine -- rank/compress/cite, never dump full files

prompt: why isnt matching on meaning alone enough to pull up the right past notes
note: Sec 6.1 why Graph-RAG over pure vector RAG -- typed edges capture what similarity misses

prompt: what stops it from telling me something that was correct long ago but has since been overruled
note: supersedes edge + structural recency -- newer knowledge overrides stale knowledge at rank time

prompt: what keeps me from writing down something thats already been captured before
note: Sec 9.4 novelty check -- 0.85 similarity gate prompts supersede/append/cancel before a new node is written

prompt: what makes this different from tools that only chart how the code hangs together
note: v2.0 positioning -- code intelligence that also remembers why, via the memory layer competitors lack

prompt: should the assistant be free to act on its own or does someone need to sign off first
note: v3 three-tier risk model (safe/confirm/danger) -- destructive tools always prompt, no allow-always escape

prompt: why did chasing the same kind of visual glitch over and over end up forcing a bigger cleanup
note: v4.x contract-first doctrine -- repeated same-shape bugs mean a missing contract, not a missing fix

prompt: would it speed things up to let the video hardware work out where everything goes
note: v4.6 scale judgment -- GPU is for rendering not layout; CPU Barnes-Hut is sub-second at this size

prompt: how do we keep an assistant from claiming the work is finished when it never really ran anything
note: v6.1.0 governance: gate honesty is evidence-based -- real captured exit codes, never the agent's word

prompt: why does writing our standards down in a document not actually make anyone follow them
note: v6.1.0 S0: written conventions are unenforced prose an agent silently skips -- the reason the governance layer exists at all

prompt: should a background service that is down be allowed to prevent me from finishing my work
note: v6.1.0 non-negotiable design principle: every hook fails open -- a checker that is unavailable must never brick the session

prompt: what does this thing know that i could not just find by looking through the files myself
note: the moat thesis: the typed graph + cross-session memory are the two things an agent's own grep+read loop cannot reconstruct

prompt: if our own numbers come out flawless what does that actually prove
note: v6.0.0 non-circularity + baseline triage: a self-derived 1.00 is a regression guard, not independent proof of correctness

prompt: why build the yardstick before trying to make anything better
note: the measure-before-tuning doctrine (v5.26 lesson): no tuning without a baseline; build the instrument first and ship it alone

prompt: what has to be true before i can honestly say a piece of work is finished
note: global-memory completion rule -- the operator-green bar

prompt: one measurement says a part has failed but nothing else agrees how should i decide
note: edge-device iron rule -- never trust a single signal to declare a fault

prompt: why does it only ever hand over a small slice of a note instead of the whole file
note: token-budgeted retrieval doctrine (<=800 tokens, never dump full files)

prompt: why not let a language model decide what kind of question is being asked
note: deliberate non-goal -- deterministic intent classification over an LLM call

prompt: how do we stop a design mistake from quietly creeping back in after it was fixed
note: guard tests as the contract's teeth against silent regression

prompt: what keeps one idea worded several different ways from filling every slot in the results
note: MMR diversity re-rank -- lambda-weighted relevance minus max similarity to already-picked hits

prompt: why do we push knowledge from other projects down the ranking instead of hiding it completely
note: v4.3.2 SOFT project isolation -- hard filter caused a silent-zero, replaced by a score multiplier

prompt: if one of the do not do this notes is written wrong does the whole assistant stop working
note: governance fail-open law -- a malformed rule degrades to non-binding and never raises

prompt: how do we know a change to search quality is a real improvement and not just a feeling
note: v5.26.0 no-tuning-without-a-baseline doctrine -- labelled set plus pure scorer

prompt: why is finding the right piece of text not the real test of whether this tool beats the assistant just looking through files itself
note: v6.0.0 moat argument -- snippet retrieval is the commodity axis; agentic task-success is the real bar

prompt: when the tool adjusts its own ranking numbers what stops it from just fitting to what it already saw
note: auto-tuner's time-ordered 80/20 train/val split -- future feedback cannot leak into training

prompt: when a file looks dodgy during a scan do we quietly ignore it or does a human get told
note: safeguards' surface-don't-silently-drop contract -- suspicious files go to a report for a user decision
